message:
fix(core): correct ungetc LIFO ordering and align strsep test expectation

stdio/file.rs - Fix ungetc to maintain LIFO (last-in-first-out) order:

The previous implementation of `StdioStream::ungetc` attempted to push
the *new* byte into the read buffer when `ungetc_byte` was already
occupied. This is incorrect because `ungetc` must preserve LIFO order:
the most recently pushed-back byte must be the next one read.

The fix changes the logic so that when a second `ungetc` call arrives
while `ungetc_byte` already holds a value, the *existing* byte is
flushed into the underlying buffer (via `buffer.unget`), and the new
byte takes its place in `ungetc_byte`. This ensures that the newest
pushed-back byte is always returned first on the next read, which is
the behavior mandated by the C standard for consecutive `ungetc` calls.

string/str.rs - Correct test expectation for strsep with no delimiter:

Commit 11f073b ("fix(abi): correct strsep to return last token and fix
next-pointer offset") changed strsep's return semantics, but the
`test_strsep_no_delimiter` test was not updated to match. The test
asserted `Some((0, 5))` when the corrected implementation now returns
`None` for this case. Update the assertion to `None` to align with the
fixed behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
